mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, leading to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months indicates rising economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Housing Starts (HOUST) over the past 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the same period, signals a strengthening housing market and a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1% increase in 3 months and UNRATE < 0.5% decrease in 3 months
regime_hint: strengthening housing market and tightening labor market
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening housing market and tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the past 3 months, combined with a 1% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over the same period, signals a tightening monetary policy and rising inflation expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.5% increase in 3 months and CPIAUCSL > 1% increase in 3 months
regime_hint: tightening monetary policy and rising inflation regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary policy and high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the past 3 months, combined with a 1% increase in the Average Hourly Earnings (not available in the observable catalog), signals a tightening labor market and rising wages, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Real Disposable Personal Income (RSAFS) over the past 3 months signals a strengthening consumer sector, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening consumer sector periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
